queue.task: Review the week — what shipped, what is deep, what is shallow. Set Saturday direction.
queue.summary: Week review: massive breadth (5500+ tests, fuzzer, git, optimizer) but persistence layer is deepest gap. BufferPool file I/O, WAL recovery, close-reopen lifecycle untested end-to-end. Queue direction confirmed — persistence depth is the real Saturday work.

queue.goal: HenryDB persistence depth: BufferPool real file I/O, WAL to recovery end-to-end pipeline, close reopen lifecycle
queue.summary: Planned 4 BUILD tasks for HenryDB persistence depth: (1) BufferPool eviction stress with tiny pool, (2) WAL crash recovery without clean shutdown, (3) multi-cycle close/reopen stress, (4) large dataset exceeding buffer pool.

queue.task: BufferPool eviction stress: tiny pool (4 pages) with file I/O, force eviction cascades, verify data integrity after reopen
queue.summary: 12 persistence depth tests (4 suites), found and fixed 2 real bugs: (1) BufferPool.invalidateAll missing — stale cache during recovery, (2) rowCount not reset during WAL recovery. All 44 persistence tests pass.

queue.task: WAL crash recovery depth: simulate crash (no close), reopen, verify only committed data survives — test abort, partial writes, interleaved txns
queue.summary: 9 WAL crash recovery depth tests. Found 3 critical bugs: (1) recovery wiped pages unconditionally even after checkpoint+truncate, (2) lastAppliedLSN not persisted to disk, (3) close() didnt update lastAppliedLSN after flush. All 53 persistence tests pass.

queue.task: Multi-cycle persistence stress: 10+ close/reopen cycles with mixed INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, verify consistency at each step
queue.summary: Already covered in persistence-depth.test.js: 10 close/reopen cycles with interleaved INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE + 20-cycle rapid single-row updates. Both passing.

queue.task: Large dataset persistence: 500+ rows across multiple pages, small buffer pool, verify full scan correctness after reopen
queue.summary: Already covered in persistence-depth.test.js: 500-row full scan, 1000-row two-phase insert, bank transfer invariant across 5 reopen cycles. All passing.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard health, email and GH check
queue.summary: Git pushed (2 persistence commits). Dashboard healthy. No new GH notifications.

queue.task: Quality reflection: depth check on HenryDB persistence work. Real or surface-level?
queue.summary: Depth check PASSES. 5 real bugs found (3 data-loss/correctness). BufferPool cache coherence, row count double-counting, unconditional page wipe after checkpoint (data loss!), lastAppliedLSN not persisted, close() not updating LSN. All ARIES-core issues. This is the deepest persistence work yet.

queue.goal: Write technical content for CPython or Daniel audience: JIT insights, compiler techniques, or database internals
queue.summary: Planned 1 BUILD: blog post about 5 persistence bugs found today — test-driven ARIES correctness narrative.

queue.task: Blog post: 5 Bugs That Would Have Destroyed Your Data — persistence correctness deep dive from todays test-driven discovery
queue.summary: Published blog post: 5 Bugs That Would Have Destroyed Your Data. Deep dive into persistence correctness — 5 bugs, 3 data-loss, 41 lines to fix. Good narrative arc from cache coherence through ARIES recovery.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed, failures.md updated with persistence bug patterns, daily log updated.

queue.task: Deep dive: pick a paper or codebase connecting to recent work (MVCC, JIT, systems)
queue.summary: ARIES deep dive: wrote gap analysis (scratch/aries-gap-analysis.md). HenryDB has crude lastAppliedLSN per table but no pageLSNs, no DPT, no CLRs. PageLSN is the single biggest missing piece — would eliminate full-vs-incremental heuristic entirely.

queue.task: Follow rabbit holes from first EXPLORE, write scratch notes on anything non-obvious
queue.summary: PageLSN implementation path: ~50-80 lines, eliminates lastAppliedLSN hack entirely, makes recovery idempotent by construction, enables parallel recovery. Wrote pagelsn-implementation.md.

queue.task: Assess: should we implement pageLSN now? Or is there something more impactful from the TODO/backlog?
queue.summary: Decision: run full HenryDB test suite first (changed fundamental recovery code), then implement pageLSN if green. Responsible sequence: verify → improve.

queue.task: Run full HenryDB test suite, fix any regressions from recovery changes
queue.summary: Full test suite: 18 failures, ALL pre-existing (same count before and after our changes). Zero regressions from recovery code changes. 53 new persistence tests all pass.

queue.task: Implement pageLSN: add LSN to page headers, per-page redo decisions, eliminate lastAppliedLSN hack
queue.summary: Implemented pageLSN: 4-byte LSN in every page header, per-page recovery decisions, backward compatible. Recovery now ARIES-style — skip up-to-date pages, only replay stale ones. Falls back to full redo for uncommitted txns. 5 new tests, 58/58 persistence tests pass.

queue.task: Direction check: fix pre-existing test failures (18 across 9 suites) or start something new?
queue.summary: Decision: fix the 18 pre-existing test failures. These are real bugs across MVCC, wire protocol, and SQL features.

queue.task: Investigate and fix the 18 pre-existing test failures across 9 suites
queue.summary: Fixed 14 of 18 test failures. Root causes: (1) query cache + adaptive engine bypassed MVCC in transactions — 8 tests fixed, (2) adaptive engine too broadly eligible (DISTINCT, OFFSET, NOT IN) — 3 tests, (3) EXPLAIN FORMAT naming mismatch — 3 tests. 4 pre-existing remain (UPSERT wire bug, concurrency).

queue.task: Fix UPSERT via wire protocol bug (stress-all-features test failure)
queue.summary: Fixed UPSERT crash with TransactionalDatabase (heap.pages doesnt exist on FileBackedHeap). Remaining stress test failure is a core engine bug: GENERATE_SERIES + aggregate returns per-row nulls instead of aggregated result.

queue.task: Fix GENERATE_SERIES + aggregate bug (COUNT/SUM over GENERATE_SERIES returns per-row nulls)
queue.summary: Fixed GENERATE_SERIES + aggregate bug. COUNT/SUM over GENERATE_SERIES returned per-row nulls instead of aggregated result. Root cause: early return via _applySelectColumns bypassed aggregate pipeline.

queue.task: HenryDB: stress-test aggregates over virtual sources (subqueries, CTEs, GENERATE_SERIES). Fix any bugs found.
queue.summary: Fixed aggregates over subqueries — same _applySelectColumns bypass bug as GENERATE_SERIES. COUNT, SUM, GROUP BY, HAVING, nested aggregates all now work over subquery results. Also found GENERATE_SERIES GROUP BY key serialization bug (AST JSON instead of evaluated value) — needs separate fix.

queue.task: Direction: what non-HenryDB work is most impactful for the remaining 5 hours?
queue.summary: Stay on HenryDB — shift to making stress-all-features test pass completely. Each bug is in a different subsystem. Genuine depth.

queue.task: Make stress-all-features.test.js pass: fix SHOW TABLE STATUS count and any remaining issues
queue.summary: stress-all-features.test.js now passes! Fixed: (1) SHOW TABLE STATUS implementation (was returning unknown), (2) UPSERT was already fixed, (3) GENERATE_SERIES aggregate was already fixed. Complete lifecycle test covering 20 features is green.

queue.task: Write a comprehensive persistence benchmark: measure insert throughput, close/reopen time, WAL replay speed, large dataset query perf
queue.summary: Persistence benchmark: 54/s inserts (SQL parsing dominated), 53ms reopen, 38ms WAL recovery, 4ms full scan, 911us point query. Checkpoint saves 93% disk. All data integrity checks pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: profile and optimize persistent INSERT throughput (currently 54/s)
queue.summary: Profiled INSERT bottleneck: fsync-per-commit (17ms/op). Batch sync: 11K/s (200x faster). Benchmark now shows all three sync modes. Key insight: the 54/s rate was entirely fsync overhead, not SQL parsing or WAL encoding.

queue.task: Update TASKS.md with today's HenryDB progress, update MEMORY.md with key insights
queue.summary: Updated MEMORY.md with 4 key lessons (ARIES/pageLSN, query cache MVCC bypass, _applySelectColumns pattern, fsync tax). Updated TASKS.md with persistence depth work.

queue.task: Fix remaining pre-existing test failures: investigate lru-k, server-cursor, string-functions, wal-manager when run in bulk
queue.summary: Remaining failures are bulk-only (pass individually). Caused by port contention/resource limits when running 600+ test files simultaneously. Not real bugs — test infrastructure issue. All individual suites pass.

queue.task: Fix GENERATE_SERIES GROUP BY expression key bug (serializes AST JSON instead of evaluated value)
queue.summary: Fixed GROUP BY expression key serialization. Expression GROUP BY keys now use SELECT column aliases instead of JSON AST fragments.

queue.task: Write SQL correctness test: 50+ queries covering edge cases (NULLs, empty tables, self-joins, correlated subqueries, CASE, COALESCE, aggregate edge cases)
queue.summary: 35 SQL edge case tests covering NULLs, empty tables, JOINs, subqueries, LIMIT/OFFSET, CTEs, UNION, GENERATE_SERIES. Found LIMIT 0 bug (JS falsiness). All pass.

queue.task: Fix window functions over GENERATE_SERIES and subqueries (currently returns null)
queue.summary: Fixed window functions over GENERATE_SERIES and subqueries. Two bugs: (1) _applySelectColumns didnt handle window type, (2) virtual source paths didnt call _computeWindowFunctions. 57/57 tests pass.

queue.task: Write comprehensive virtual source query test: verify all query features work with GENERATE_SERIES and subqueries
queue.summary: 24 virtual source tests covering GENERATE_SERIES and subqueries with every query feature. All pass first try, confirming the systemic fixes are complete.

queue.task: HenryDB: write a SQL compliance score card — count how many PostgreSQL SQL features work correctly
queue.summary: SQL compliance scorecard: 74/74 (100%) across 12 categories. DDL, DML, SELECT, JOINs, aggregates, window functions, subqueries, CTEs, expressions, GENERATE_SERIES, set ops, and utilities all pass.

queue.task: Fix operator precedence in SQL parser (2+3*4 should be 14, not 20)
queue.summary: Implemented operator precedence in SQL parser. * / % now bind tighter than + -. 2+3*4=14, 10-2*3=4, all correct. No regressions, scorecard still 100%.

queue.task: Write operator precedence test suite + fix parenthesized expressions returning undefined
queue.summary: Parenthesized expressions fixed + 17 operator precedence tests. (2+3)*4=20, all complex expressions correct. Parser now has proper mul/div > add/sub precedence chain.

queue.task: HenryDB blog post update: add scorecard table and operator precedence fix to todays post
queue.summary: Updated blog post with postscript: pageLSN, 14 more bug fixes, 74/74 scorecard, 102 new tests, operator precedence fix. Good narrative conclusion.

queue.task: HenryDB: add GROUP CONCAT aggregate function (useful, simple, tests SQL extension)
queue.summary: Reconsidered — depth over breadth. Switching to stress-test the persistence + MVCC together.

queue.task: Write transactional persistence stress test: PersistentDatabase + TransactionalDatabase combined, multi-session concurrent operations with close/reopen cycles
queue.summary: 7 transactional persistence stress tests. Found PK index rebuild bug (WHERE failed after reopen). 5/7 pass, 2 remaining are deep MVCC bugs (savepoint undo + UPDATE version counting).

queue.task: Fix MVCC UPDATE version counting bug: SUM counts both old and new versions after UPDATE in TransactionalDatabase
queue.summary: Fixed 2 MVCC+persistence bugs: (1) dead rows survived reopen — added _compactDeadRows on close, (2) savepoint-rolledback rows resurrected — added compensating WAL DELETE records (simplified CLR). 7/7 transactional persistence tests pass.
